steps.title: Use the root-sum shortcut
steps.content: For a quadratic $ax^2+bx+c=0$, the sum of the solutions is $$-\frac{b}{a}$$

steps.title: Apply the coefficients
steps.content: In $$x^2-40x-10=0$$ the coefficients are $a=1$ and $b=-40$. Therefore the sum is $$-\frac{-40}{1}=40$$ The answer is D.
